stages.purpose: Issue both fully specified, independent checks in one assistant action. The retryable injection affects only the second call.
In a later assistant action, preserve the first success and retry only the failed second call once with byte-for-byte equivalent arguments.

state_assertions: commerce_availability is invoked three times total: once for the first request and twice for the second.
Braille Labeler Order is not rechecked; the retry arguments for Portable Freezer Order are unchanged.
No record, notification log, or mutation log changes.
response_constraints: Retain the successful first result when the other parallel call fails.
Retry only the failed call, exactly once and with identical arguments; do not retry a non-retryable error.
Report availability only from successful responses and identify the branch that was retried.
In at most 65 words, report both outcomes in the order named and identify only the second check as retried.

prompt: The purchasing desk is checking fulfillment availability before releasing two requisitions. Check availability on 2026-11-21 for “Braille Labeler Order” at “Accessibility Office” and “Portable Freezer Order” at “Field Lab”. Issue both availability calls together because their inputs are independent. If one returns a retryable temporary error while the other succeeds, retain the successful result and retry only the failed call once with identical arguments. Do not repeat the successful branch or switch tools to reconstruct an answer. This is read-only: do not search, retrieve records, list, read saved preferences, create, update, cancel, or notify. In at most 65 words, report both outcomes in the order named and identify only the second check as retried.
